name: A2 细胞的多样性（原核/真核）
name_en: A2 Origins of cells & cell diversity
subject: biology
domain_name: 主题 A · 统一性与多样性 Unity and Diversity
curriculum_points:
A1.2.6—DNA as a double helix made of two antiparallel strands of nucleotides with two strands linked by hydrogen bonding between complementary base pairs
In diagrams of DNA structure, students should draw the two strands antiparallel, but are not required to draw the helical shape. Students should show adenine (A) paired with thymine (T), and guanine (G) paired with cytosine (C). Students are not required to memorize the relative lengths of the purine and pyrimidine bases, or the numbers of hydrogen bonds.

A1.2.8—Role of complementary base pairing in allowing genetic information to be replicated and expressed
Students should understand that complementarity is based on hydrogen bonding.

A1.2.12—Purine-to-pyrimidine bonding as a component of DNA helix stability
Adenine–thymine (A–T) and cytosine–guanine (C–G) pairs have equal length, so the DNA helix has the same three-dimensional structure, regardless of the base sequence.

errors.description: 只记年代与名词，不能用史料与制度变迁解释历史进程。
把传说叙述与考古/文献证据混为一谈，未区分证据强度。

exercises.stem: 结合课标要求，说明「A2 细胞的多样性（原核/真核）」的核心内涵，并各举一条史料或史实依据。
exercises.answer: 应答扣课标要点：A1.2.6—DNA as a double helix made of two antiparallel strands of nucleotides with two strands linked by hydrogen bonding…；并区分证据类型（文献/考古/制度等）。

exercises.stem: 用因果链解释：A1.2.8—Role of complementary base pairing in allowing genetic information to be … 对理解「A2 细胞的多样性（原核/真核）」为何重要？
exercises.answer: 需写出前因—制度/经济/思想变化—后果，避免只罗列年代。
